## Read examples

Requester-pays prerequisite (once public IAM permits it):

```bash
export BILLING_PROJECT='<your-gcp-billing-project>'
LOCAL_DIR='./parquet-catalog-data/edges__cell_line_expresses_gene'
rm -rf -- "$LOCAL_DIR"
mkdir -p "$LOCAL_DIR"
gcloud storage cp --billing-project="$BILLING_PROJECT" 'gs://jouvencekb/kg/v2/edges/cell_line_expresses_gene.parquet' "$LOCAL_DIR/"
```

PyArrow (GCS credentials/application-default credentials must carry the billing project):

```python
import os
import gcsfs
import pyarrow.dataset as ds
billing_project = os.environ['BILLING_PROJECT']
fs = gcsfs.GCSFileSystem(project=billing_project, requester_pays=billing_project)
paths = sorted(fs.glob('jouvencekb/kg/v2/edges/cell_line_expresses_gene.parquet'))
dataset = ds.dataset(paths, filesystem=fs, format='parquet')
print(dataset.head(5, columns=['x_id']))
```

DuckDB:

```sql
-- Run after the requester-pays `gcloud storage cp` command above.
SELECT "x_id" FROM read_parquet('./parquet-catalog-data/edges__cell_line_expresses_gene/cell_line_expresses_gene.parquet') ORDER BY "x_id" NULLS LAST LIMIT 5;
```

## LaminDB / PyG linkage

`x_id` and `y_id` resolve against canonical node tables. Eligible biological relations become PyG edge stores; metadata-only relations must remain excluded from default training exports.

## Caveats and leakage

Construct train/validation/test splits before exposing relation or evidence fields. Evidence counts, predicates, scores and source text can leak held-out edge labels.
